api: Spotler CRM (Really Simple Systems) API v4 (https://apiv4.reallysimplesystems.com)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the real, live CRM API v4 ("Spotler CRM" is Really Simple Systems' rebrand; its actual public REST API is CRM API v4, documented at https://support.reallysimplesystems.com/api-v4/ — the docs_url this bundle's wave2 metadata.json previously declared, https://api.spotler.com/, does not resolve to any API reference at all). This pass discovered the bundle's wave2 host/auth/envelope assumptions (https://api.spotlercrm.com/api/v1, X-API-Key header, flat {data:[...]} response) do not match the real API (https://apiv4.reallysimplesystems.com, OAuth2 Bearer access token, {metadata,list:[{metadata,record}]} response envelope) at all; the 4 wave2 streams are retained UNCHANGED at their existing (real-API-incorrect) shape per the meta-rule against altering already-migrated behavior — see Known limits in docs.md. This pass adds 3 new streams (activities, campaigns, cases) against the REAL API's own documented object catalog (accounts/activities/contacts/campaigns/campaigndetails/campaignstages/cases/documents/opportunities/opportunityhistories/opportunity_lines, per the API's own overview page), using a newly-added access_token secret and the real host/envelope; the remaining 5 real objects (campaigndetails, campaignstages, documents, opportunityhistories, opportunity_lines) are excluded for this pass since their per-field record shape could not be confirmed with reasonable confidence from the publicly available documentation (only the accounts object's full field list is illustrated in the API overview's worked example; every other object's fields are named only, not enumerated) — shipping a guessed schema for them was judged worse than not shipping the stream at all. No writes were added: this bundle's real API v4 fully supports POST/PATCH/DELETE per its own docs, but every new stream in this pass uses an absolute stream.path (the only way to reach the real API host given the existing base_url targets a different, incorrect host), and write actions have no conformance skip-marker equivalent (write_request_shape's capture-server replay always points b.HTTP.URL at the test double, which an absolute-URL action.path bypasses entirely) — so untestable write actions were not added, matching the reasoning squarespace's docs.md records for the same host-mismatch class of problem.

excluded.reason: no conformance skip-marker equivalent exists for write actions targeting an absolute stream.path (the real CRM API v4 host, distinct from this bundle's base_url) — write_request_shape's capture-server replay always points b.HTTP.URL at the test double, which an absolute-URL action.path bypasses entirely, so an untestable write action was not added

the opportunities list stream's own real API supports an inline lines=true request parameter that embeds this exact object under each opportunity's own lines key (per the API's documented Opportunity Lines section) rather than requiring a separate list endpoint call; already reachable through the opportunities object, not modeled as its own stream in this pass
returns the field-name/type/required-ness metadata FOR an object type, not the object's own data records
